title: Stigmatopteris guianensis
taxonomy: Stigmatopteris guianensis is a species of fern in the family Dryopteridaceae. It is a member of the genus Stigmatopteris, which is a group of ferns that are commonly found in tropical and subtropical regions. The family Dryopteridaceae is a large and diverse group of ferns that are found in many parts of the world. The classification of Stigmatopteris guianensis places it within the kingdom Plantae, phylum Pteridophyta, and class Polypodiopsida. This classification reflects its status as a type of vascular plant that reproduces via spores.

intro: Stigmatopteris guianensis, also known as the Guianan shield fern, is a species of fern that belongs to the family Dryopteridaceae. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which can help to alleviate symptoms of conditions such as arthritis, wounds, and infections. The bioactive constituents of Stigmatopteris guianensis include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, the Guianan shield fern is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat a range of health conditions, including fever, cough, and digestive issues.
